perturbation.original_text: Sponsor will be the exclusive retail music store sponsor of the Excite Site and the Excite Broadcast Pages. Excite will not permit the display of advertising banners, promotional buttons, promotional links or other promotional materials for any retail sale of Music Products on the Excite Site, except those Music Products offered by Sponsor, nor advertising by any other Retail Music Store. "Retail Music Store" shall mean any entity which is primarily engaged in the sale of Music Products at retail to consumers.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor will be the exclusive retail music store sponsor of the Excite Site and the Excite Broadcast Pages, unless Excite deems it necessary to display advertising banners, promotional buttons, promotional links or other promotional materials for any retail sale of Music Products on the Excite Site, even those Music Products not offered by Sponsor, and may permit advertising by any other Retail Music Store at its discretion. "Retail Music Store" shall mean any entity which is primarily engaged in the sale of Music Products at retail to consumers.
perturbation.explanation: The original text guarantees exclusivity for the Sponsor. The modified text allows Excite to override this exclusivity at its discretion, directly contradicting the original exclusivity clause and creating uncertainty about the sponsor's rights. The addition of "unless Excite deems it necessary" and "and may permit advertising by any other Retail Music Store at its discretion" allows Excite to bypass the exclusivity agreement outlined in the first sentence. This introduces an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor will be the exclusive retail music store sponsor of the Excite Site and the Excite Broadcast Pages. Excite will not permit the display of advertising banners, promotional buttons, promotional links or other promotional materials for any retail sale of Music Products on the Excite Site, except those Music Products offered by Sponsor, nor advertising by any other Retail Music Store. "Retail Music Store" shall mean any entity which is primarily engaged in the sale of Music Products at retail to consumers. "Music Products" 2 CONFIDENTIAL shall mean pre-recorded music hard goods, digitally distributed music (except live and/or cybercast events), music books (to the extent this Agreement does not conflict with Excite's existing agreement with Amazon.com), music-related t-shirts and apparel, and music videos (except live and/or cybercast events). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Excite may make available opportunities on the Excite Site to purchase Music Products from parties other than Sponsor if such Music Products are not available from Sponsor so long as, prior to entering into arrangements to make available opportunities to purchase Music Products from parties other than Sponsor, Excite notifies Sponsor of its interest in the Music Products and gives Sponsor thirty (30) days to make the desired Music Products available through the Sponsor Site. Excite and Sponsor acknowledge that certain market opportunities may arise in which the desired Music Products must be made available on less than thirty (30) days advance notice and will work together in good faith to maximize those opportunities. In no event will Excite enter into arrangements to make available opportunities to purchase Music Products from parties other than Sponsor that would prevent Sponsor from being the exclusive source of such Music Products on the Excite Site once the Music Products become available through Sponsor.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text both explicitly state that the Sponsor will be the 'exclusive retail music store sponsor' and that Excite will not permit advertising by any other Retail Music Store. The changed text directly contradicts this exclusivity by granting Excite the discretion to display advertising for other retail music stores, thereby creating a direct and irreconcilable conflict. The phrase 'unless Excite deems it necessary' in the changed text directly undermines the exclusivity promised in the contradicted text, rendering the Sponsor's exclusive rights uncertain and unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Excite will report traffic, Impressions and click-thrus to Sponsor on a monthly basis.
perturbation.changed_text: Excite will report traffic, Impressions and click-thrus to Sponsor on a monthly basis, only if Sponsor requests such reports in writing, and even then, the accuracy of the reported data is not guaranteed.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Excite *will* report traffic data monthly. The modified text introduces a condition (Sponsor's written request) and disclaims any guarantee of accuracy, creating a contradiction. Now, it's uncertain whether Excite is actually obligated to provide these reports monthly, and if they do, whether the data is reliable. This affects Sponsor's ability to track the effectiveness of the sponsorship. the initial sentence states the action that will take place, but the addition creates uncertainity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Excite will report traffic, Impressions and click-thrus to Sponsor on a monthly basis.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text both state that "Excite *will* report traffic, Impressions and click-thrus to Sponsor on a monthly basis." However, the changed text introduces a condition requiring a written request from the Sponsor for these reports and disclaims the accuracy of the data. This creates a contradiction because the initial, and contradicted, statements mandate monthly reporting without conditions, whereas the modified text makes reporting conditional and potentially unreliable. This qualifies as a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction, as the same clause appears in multiple locations (original and contradicted text), but the altered version introduces conflicting implications regarding Excite's obligation.

perturbation.original_text: All data concerning users and their behavior relating to the use of the Excite Site will be owned solely by Excite. Data relating to the use of Sponsor's Site (excluding users referred from the Excite Site) will be owned solely by Sponsor. Data relating to the use of the Co-Branded Pages will be jointly owned and shared by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: All data concerning users and their behavior relating to the use of the Excite Site will be owned solely by Excite, except for any data that Excite, at its sole discretion, deems relevant to the Co-Branded Pages, which will be jointly owned and shared. Data relating to the use of Sponsor's Site (excluding users referred from the Excite Site) will be owned solely by Sponsor. Data relating to the use of the Co-Branded Pages will be jointly owned and shared by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by giving Excite the ability to selectively designate Excite Site user data as jointly owned if it is relevant to the Co-Branded Pages. This undermines the original clear distinction of data ownership. Now it is not clear what user data are owned solely by Excite. The term all implies complete exclusion, but the change affects this inclusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All data concerning users and their behavior relating to the use of the Excite Site will be owned solely by Excite. Data relating to the use of Sponsor's Site (excluding users referred from the Excite Site) will be owned solely by Sponsor. Data relating to the use of the Co-Branded Pages will be jointly owned and shared by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by allowing Excite to selectively designate data originally defined as solely owned by Excite as jointly owned. This conflicts with the original statement: 'All data concerning users and their behavior relating to the use of the Excite Site will be owned solely by Excite' found in both the original and contradicted texts. The addition of 'except for any data that Excite, at its sole discretion, deems relevant to the Co-Branded Pages, which will be jointly owned and shared' undermines the exclusiveness of the ownership.
